Analysis.description: An analysis that can contain one or more queries. The "id" is a string field that can be used by the client to identify the analysis (although it's not used by the server for any special purpose). "visualization_attributes" is a dictionary that also can be used by the client for storing meta information for visualization purposes, but doesn't change anything in the data processing in the server.

AnalysisCompositeFilter.description: A composite filter with its responses.

AnalysisCustomSectorAllBrands.description: A custom sector expanded into its composing brands.

AnalysisDemographicFilter.description: A demographic filter with its values. Although not exposed in the specification, it needs a "values" field containing a single integer or a list of integers.

AnalysisEventsResponse.description: List of events for an analysis.

AnalysisExport.description: This is an export of an analysis.

AnalysisExportRequest.description: This is the request for an analysis export.

AnalysisIndustryAllBrands.description: An industry expanded into its composing brands.

AnalysisListResponse.description: This is a response that represents an list of analyses.

AnalysisMetricFilter.description: A metric filter. Although not exposed in the specification, it needs a "values" field containing a single integer or a list of integers.

AnalysisProfilesFilter.description: A Profiles filter with its values. The definition_name must be composed by <dataseries>__<definition> - e.g. "profiles_us__age". Although not exposed in the specification, it needs a "values" field containing a single string or a list of strings.

AnalysisQuery.description: One single query inside an analysis. It corresponds to a series in a chart, for example.

entity.description: An entity that will be represented in the query. If an entity with "AllBrands" is used, it will be expanded to one resulting query per brand, generating multiple queries as the outcome of an analysis execution.

filters.description: A list of filters to use in the query. Each item in the list must be one of the possible types of filters (they can be mixed in the list), and they're applied in the query with the "AND" logic (all filters are applied), but each value in a filter is applied with the "OR" logic within the context of that same filter.

id.description: An optional string that can be used by the client to identify each query in an analysis.

is_market_scanner.description: Read-only. Whether the query is related to a Market Scanner subscription or not.

metrics_score_types.description: A mapping to define which score type to use for each metric desired in the query.

moving_average.description: The moving average (AKA "rolling mean") that should be applied to the query execution. Leading dates are also used to calculate the datapoint of the start date of the query.

period.description: The period for which the data should be fetched. The moving average doesn't affect the start of the period, although it does get used for calculating the first datapoint as well.

properties_meta.description: Read-only. Properties usable to get labels applicable to properties of this query.

title.description: An optional string to represent the title of the analysis.

uuid.description: Read-only. If coming from a saved analysis, it represents the UUID of the query.

visualization_attributes.description: Read-only. Special attributes defined by the UI for visualization purposes.

AnalysisQueryResult.description: This is a processing result for one query inside an analysis. It contains the data processed by the server as a result for doing the analyses themselves.

AnalysisRequest.description: This is a request that represents an analysis, and it can be used to execute the analysis itself on the server.

AnalysisResponse.description: This is a response that represents an analysis, and it can be used to describe the analysis and its properties.

AnalysisResult.description: This is a processing result for an analysis that contains one or more queries results.

AnalysisResultResponse.description: This is the response for an analysis result.

AnalysisSectorAllBrands.description: A sector expanded into its composing brands.

BinaryPeriod.description: Period with a start and an end date.

CompositeFilterRequestResponse.description: This is a request that represents a composite filter along with its possible responses.

CompositeFilterResponse.description: This is one possible response from a composite filter.

CompositeFiltersResponse.description: This is a response with a list of composite filters.

ConfidenceBound.description: A confidence bound to apply to all calculated values. The type can be "upper" or "lower", and the percent can be any integer between 1 and 99.

ConfidenceBoundType.description: The confidence bound type, if "upper" or "lower".

CustomSectorRequestResponse.description: Successful request or response body schema for custom sector.

CustomSectorsResponse.description: Successful response body schema for custom sectors.

DemographicFilterDefinition.description: Demographic filter definition, will all its possible values.

DemographicFiltersResponse.description: Successful response for getting demographic filters definitions.

EntitySearchResponse.description: Successful response for searching for entities.

EventsResponse.description: Successful response for list of events.

FilterExpression.description: A filter expression. It uses the same syntax as the composite filters responses. **Only use this if you know what you're doing, as the syntax can be tricky**.

FilterExpressionDefinition.description: This is a definition for a filter expression.

FilterOp.description: The operators supported in filter definitions.

Frequency.description: A frequency, normally used for resampling data in periods. If the data should be resampled to 2-month chunks, you can use a Frequency of size "2" and of type "month_end", for example.

FrequencyType.description: A type of frequency. The types that end in "_from_day" define periods congruent to a start date, when it's defined. For example, if an analysis is done from 2018-01-15 to 2018-12-15, using "month_from_day" will set the first period from 2018-01-15 until 2018-02-14, the second from 2018-02-15 to 2018-03-14 and so on.

IndustriesResponse.description: Successful request or response body schema for industries.

Industry.description: An industry with possible children industries

IndustryRequestResponse.description: Successful request or response body schema for industry.

Meta.description: Metadata for any request and response to and from the server. Currently only the version is included.

Metric.description: One of the available metrics to use in analyses.

MetricFilterDefinition.description: Definition of a metric filter, with all its possible values.

MetricFilterType.description: The type of a metric filter, which affects which options can be chosen for filtering analyses queries.

MetricFiltersResponse.description: Successful response for getting metric filters.

OffsetPeriod.description: Period normally representing a sentence, like "last week" etc. The example here means "last month". Can receive an "adjust_by" relative date to push the period forward.

OperationResponse.description: Simple response for operations that don't return usable data.

Period.description: Period with start and end dates

ProfilesCategoriesAndDefinitions.description: Profiles subcategories and definitions that are available inside a Profiles category.

ProfilesCategoriesResponse.description: Successful response for Profiles categories and definitions.

ProfilesDataseries.description: A dataseries and its definitions, including languages it's avalailable to.

ProfilesDataseriesResponse.description: Successful response for Profiles dataseries lists.

ProfilesDefinition.description: A Profiles definition with its codes and categories where it's available at.

ProfilesDefinitionResponse.description: Successful response for a specific Profiles definition.

ProfilesPartialDefinition.description: A simpler form of a Profiles definition.

Ranking.description: A ranking definition for later execution. The "id" is a string field that can be used by the client to identify the ranking.

RankingExport.description: This is an export of a ranking.

RankingExportRequest.description: This is the request for a ranking export.

Region.description: Region with name and possible label.

RelativeDate.description: How many days, weeks, months and/or years ago.

ScoreType.description: The score type to use. "net_score" means both positives and negatives and neutrals affect the score. "positives" means only positives count, negatives and neutrals are discarded. "negatives" means only negatives count. "neutrals" means only neutrals count. "positives_neutrals" means positives and neutrals count. "negatives_neutrals" means negatives and neutrals count.

Scoring.description: The scoring population to use. "total" means it uses all the population. "aware" means it excludes unaware respondents. "opinion" means it excludes unaware and neutral respondents.

SearchSector.description: Sector with region and possible label.

Sector.description: Sector with region and possible label.

SectorResponse.description: Successful response body schema for sector.

SectorsResponse.description: Successful response body schema for sectors.

SignificanceDiff.description: This is a significance difference object for all score types. 1 means there was a significant change to higher value. -1 means significantly lower value. 0 means no change.

SignificanceTestRequest.description: This is the request for a significance test.

SignificanceTestResponse.description: This is the response for a significance test.

SignificanceTestsRequest.description: This is the request for batch significance tests.

SignificanceTestsResponse.description: This is the response for batch significance tests.

StringResponse.description: Just a simple response with a string.

TagResourcesResponse.description: This is a response that represents resources for a tag

TagResponse.description: This is a response that represents a tag

TagsResponse.description: This is a response that represents a list of tags

UserCredentialsRequest.description: A request to get authenticated user by their credentials.

VisualizationAttributes.description: A dictionary with possible visualization attributes. Only `type` is validated, the rest of the attributes are open.
